blocks.text: Be pleased, O God, to deliver me. O Lord, make haste to help me!

Ps 70: A prayer that the psalmist’s persecutors would be defeated. An individual petition that enemies be punished (vv. 1–3) and God‐seekers experience the joy of salvation (vv. 4–5). It is also found with a few variants in Ps 40.13–17.
Let those be put to shame and confusion who seek my life. Let those be turned back and brought to dishonor who desire to hurt me.
2 : , violating accepted morality brought dishonor upon the violator. The prayer is that the enemies’ schemes be publicly frustrated.
Let those who say, “Aha, Aha!” turn back because of their shame.
1–3 : , and , echoed in the final lines, bringing closure to the poem.
Let all who seek you rejoice and be glad in you. Let those who love your salvation say evermore, “God is great!”
4 : God’s help of this individual will encourage other righteous people.
But I am poor and needy; hasten to me, O God! You are my help and my deliverer; O Lord, do not delay!
5 : Psalms often depicts the supplicant as and needy as in the previous psalm.
